data.title: South Korea’s ruling democratic party launches crypto task force
data.description:
South Korea’s ruling Democratic Party (DP) has launched a new crypto task force to draft policies for the sector.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/korea-party-launches-crypto-task-force/

data.title: Arthur Hayes Family Office Awards Bitcoin Development Grant to Spherostrat
data.description: According to PANews, Maelstrom, the family office of Arthur Hayes, has announced a one-year Bitcoin development grant to Spherostrat. The grant will enable Spherostrat to focus on enhancing privacy in peer-to-peer networks, optimizing block validation, and developing DLEQ proofs for Silent Payment addresses.

The address currently holds a total of 50 million ASTER (worth approximately $115.5 million), with a paper profit of approximately $9.92 million.

data.title: GriffinAI (GAIN) plummets 90% to $0.006
data.description: According to market data, the price of GriffinAI (GAIN) has plummeted by 90% to $0.006, which may be related to its issuance of 5 billion additional tokens.

data.title: Whale's Recent Ethereum Long Position Faces Narrow Margin
data.description: According to Foresight News, a major cryptocurrency trader, previously incurring a $35.84 million loss on a long Ethereum (ETH) position and a $7.5 million loss on a short Bitcoin (BTC) position, has recently opened a new long position on Ethereum. The trader acquired 15,700 ETH, valued at approximately $65.4 million, with an entry price of $4,178 and a liquidation price of $4,078, leaving a narrow margin of $100.

data.title: Seedify was attacked by North Korean hackers, and cross-chain contracts were used to illegally issue additional SFUND
data.description: Seedify officially disclosed that the private key of one of its developers was stolen by a North Korean state-affiliated hacker group at approximately 12:05 UTC today. The attackers exploited this to modify the OFT contract settings, illegally issuing a large number of SFUND tokens via a cross-chain bridge contract on the Avalanche chain. These tokens were then transferred to Ethereum, Arbitrum, and Base. The attackers then exploited the liquidity on these chains and sold the largest amount on the BNB chain, ultimately allowing the team to take control. Seedify stated that the incident was limited to the minting permissions of the stolen private key; core contracts, user wallets, the official website, and the underlying protocol were not affected. The team has suspended all cross-chain bridges and is working with multiple centralized exchanges to freeze the suspected funds and revoke related permissions. Officials emphasized that BNB's on-chain liquidity is no longer at risk, but cautioned users not to purchase SFUND on other chains for the time being.

data.title: Tether plans to raise up to $20 billion at a $500 billion valuation
data.description: According to people familiar with the matter, stablecoin issuer Tether Holdings is in talks with investors to raise $15 billion to $20 billion through a private placement, representing approximately 3% of the company. If the transaction is completed, the company's valuation could reach $500 billion, making it one of the world's most valuable private companies. (Jinshi)

data.title: Australia Proposes Regulatory Framework for Crypto Platforms
data.description:
According to PANews, the Australian government has unveiled a draft regulation aimed at extending financial services laws to the cryptocurrency sector. The proposal mandates that operators obtain an Australian Financial Services License and establishes two categories: digital asset platforms and custody platforms. These platforms are required to manage conflicts of interest, provide dispute resolution mechanisms, and adhere to custody and settlement standards.

The new regulations encompass packaged tokens, public token infrastructure, and staking services, granting regulatory bodies the flexibility to adjust their powers. Additionally, the Australian Securities and Investments Commission has already implemented a licensing exemption for stablecoin intermediaries. The proposed legislation is currently open for public consultation.

data.title: SBF X account posted again after half a year

data.title: US Senate Prepares October 1 Hearing on Crypto Taxation
data.description:
The US Senate Finance Committee announced that it will hold a hearing on October 1 to examine how digital assets should be taxed. Industry leaders, including Coinbase, are expected to provide testimony on taxation challenges and potential legislative reforms. Senate Gathers Industry Feedback on Tax Update On Wednesday, Senate Finance Committee Chairman Mike Crapo confirmed that the hearing, titled “Examining the Taxation of Digital Assets,” will feature testimony from Lawrence Zlatkin, Coinbase Global’s vice president of tax, and Jason Somensatto, director of policy at Coin Center. The session will explore the practical implementation of tax reporting, asset classification, and potential reforms to simplify compliance
source: https://beincrypto.com/us-senate-prepares-october-1-hearing-on-crypto-taxation/

data.title: CleanSpark Faces Class Action Lawsuit Over Alleged Misstatements
data.description: According to PANews, a federal court in the Southern District of New York has ruled that investors of CleanSpark Inc. can proceed with a class action lawsuit against the company. The lawsuit alleges that CleanSpark made false statements during its transition from clean energy to Bitcoin mining. The court dismissed CleanSpark's argument that individual issues should take precedence over common ones and found that the company's defense regarding investor reliance does not apply at the class certification stage. The court also recognized Darshan Hasthantra as a representative for the plaintiffs.

data.title: US Senate Finance Committee to discuss crypto tax matters next week
data.description:

source: https://coindoo.com/chainlink-taps-into-canton-network-backed-by-global-banks/

data.title: Cathie Wood takes first stake in Alibaba in four years, worth over $16 million
data.description: Cathie Wood, known as "Wood Sister," has reopened her Alibaba (BABA.N) fund for the first time in four years. According to the daily trading report released by Wood's Ark Investment Management, two of its ETFs purchased Alibaba ADRs on Monday, totaling approximately $16.3 million. On Tuesday, Alibaba ADRs hit their highest level since November 2021, nearly doubling year-to-date. According to the SEC's search tool, Ark's first investment in Alibaba dates back to 2014, shortly after the company went public that year. However, no records of investments or proxy voting have been found since September 2021. This latest investment may signal a renewed expansion of Ark's investments in the Chinese internet sector.

data.title: The US CFTC launches a tokenized collateral program to allow the use of stablecoins in derivatives transactions
data.description: Acting Chairwoman Caroline D. Pham of the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) announced the launch of a "tokenized collateral" initiative, allowing derivatives traders to use non-cash assets such as stablecoins as collateral to improve market efficiency and transparency. This initiative builds on a pilot program launched in February of this year with Circle, Coinbase, Crypto.com, Ripple, and Moonpay. The industry is invited to submit comments by October 20th. This move is seen as a significant step forward by the CFTC in modernizing capital markets and clarifying crypto regulation.

data.title: Fold partners with Stripe and Visa to launch Bitcoin rewards credit card
data.description: Nasdaq-listed company Fold (FLD) announced a partnership with Stripe and Visa to launch a Bitcoin rewards credit card. Running on the Visa network, users will receive 2% instant Bitcoin cash back on every purchase, increasing to 3.5% when paying with a Fold checking account and up to 10% at select participating retailers. The card requires no collateral or exchange account, and rewards are automatically distributed in Bitcoin.

data.title: The probability of the Federal Reserve cutting interest rates by 25 basis points in October is 93%.
data.description: According to CME's "Fed Watch," the probability of the Fed keeping interest rates unchanged in October is 7%, and the probability of a 25 basis point rate cut is 93%. The probability of the Fed keeping interest rates unchanged in December is 1.3%, the probability of a cumulative 25 basis point rate cut is 22.5%, and the probability of a cumulative 50 basis point rate cut is 76.2%.
